question_title: Will ABNB's market close price on 2026-04-10 be higher than its market close price on 2026-04-03?
summary: ABNB is in a bearish regime: down 8% over 20 days with a -6.25% shock on Mar 27 from which it has only partially recovered. Monte Carlo simulations using recent parameters give 34-44% probability of being up over 5 trading days, while conditional base rates from similar drawdown/shock conditions give 46-55%. The macro environment (tariff uncertainty, S&P 500 weakness) and Airbnb-specific headwinds ($2.5B bond offering, travel demand concerns) add downward pressure. High daily volatility (2.25%) keeps the probability from straying too far from 50%. Final estimate: 45% probability that ABNB closes higher on Apr 10 than Apr 3.

factors.description: Negative recent momentum: 20-day return -8%, 5-day return -4.7%, strongly bearish trend
Post-shock conditional base rate: after >=5% single-day drops, ABNB is positive over next 5 days only 46.3% of the time (n=41)
Macro headwinds: tariff uncertainty (Section 122 at 10-15%), trade war legal battles, S&P 500 down 6% from 52w high
Airbnb-specific headwinds: $2.5B bond offering increasing cost of capital, inflation/energy costs pressuring travel demand
Drawdown conditional base rate: when ABNB is >=10% below 52w high, 5-day forward returns are positive 54.8% of the time (n=155)
Monte Carlo simulation with 60-day regime parameters gives 44.3% probability of being up over 5 days
High daily volatility (2.25%) means wide outcome distribution - close to coin flip territory despite negative drift

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.45 (45% probability ABNB closes higher on Apr 10 than Apr 3)**

**Key findings:**

- **Bearish momentum**: ABNB is down 8% over 20 days and 4.7% over 5 days, with a -6.25% shock on Mar 27 (linked to broader market sell-off and Airbnb's $2.5B bond offering)
- **Conditional base rates**: After comparable single-day drops of ≥5%, ABNB is positive over 5 days only 46.3% of the time (n=41). During ≥10% drawdowns from 52w high, it's positive 54.8% of the time (n=155)
- **Monte Carlo simulations**: Using recent 20-day regime parameters → 34% up; 60-day → 44%; full-year → 52%
- **Macro context**: Tariff uncertainty persists (Section 122 at 10-15%), S&P 500 is down 6% from 52w high, and trade war legal battles continue
- **High volatility** (2.25% daily) keeps the probability close to 50% despite negative drift — outcomes are wide-ranging over just 5 trading days

The negative momentum and macro headwinds pull the probability below 50%, but the short time horizon and high volatility prevent it from going much lower.
sources_consulted: [Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) Stock Price, News, Quote & History - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ABNB/)

[Tariff Turmoil 2.0: Markets Reeling as Global Trade War Enters High-Stakes New Chapter](https://markets.financialcontent.com/stocks/article/marketminute-2026-3-2-tariff-turmoil-20-markets-reeling-as-global-trade-war-enters-high-stakes-new-chapter)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the latest market close price of ABNB. If it is higher than the close price on 2026-04-03, the question will resolve to 'Yes'. The close price will be pulled from the Yahoo Finance API.
fine_print: Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price.
